videos.title: Community Connection
videos.description: Cascade Crest Nordic Race http://VisitBend.com/Events, High Desert Museum Science Party http://HighDesertMuseum.org, Caldera Arts Open Studio http://CalderaArts.org
videos.transcript:
here's your Community Connection brought
to you by kohd 9 and Les Schwab Tires
ski your favorite Nordic Trails inside
and outside the Mount Bachelor trail
system this Saturday at the Cascade
Crest Nordic ski race benefiting mbsef
explore the forces of our universe at
the High Desert Museum's science party
starting this Saturday there'll be two
shows daily during spring break also on
Saturday Artisan residents at Caldera
Arts Center in sisters open their
Studios to share the wonderful work
they've created

videos.transcript:
any time that I've given you the
impression that you just can't afford to
be outdoors let's just say I'm sorry
like a few weeks ago when I did a video
all about winter layers and if you add
up all the layers that I talked about it
was well over a thousand dollars and
that was just for the clothes that I'm
wearing we're not talking about boots or
any of the other gear that you might
need but you know what you don't have to
spend that much money and to prove it I
decided I was going to buy all the same
layers everything you need to manage
sweat properly in cold weather but this
time I was going to do it for under two
hundred dollars for everything so let's
check it out okay so what I want to do
is I want to show you these expensive
layers that obviously I've chosen for
the trips that I want to do but I also
want to show you some extremely
affordable Alternatives that will work
just as well at a fraction of the cost
but first we got to talk about what
winter layering is and what it's not so
winter layering isn't really about
staying warm as much as it's about
managing sweat if you get wet when it's
cold out you are going to be cold and
when you're moving uphill you're going
to get wet from sweat especially if
you're wearing a lot of layers so
layering systems are designed to manage
that sweat you put on a layer when you
aren't moving very much like when you're
at camp or on extended brakes and you
take off layers when you're exerting
yourself but even with just one or two
layers it's possible to sweat with high
energy activities like snowshoeing
backpacking and Hiking so that's when
special layering features come in handy
and they don't have to be that expensive
like the outer shell you can spend 200
to 400 on high-tech Fabrics like
Gore-Tex which is probably the most
breathable waterproof fabric on the
market but two to four hundred dollars
is just for one layer and we want to
spend less than that for all of our
layers and Shell's main purpose is just
to keep wind and wet snow out while also
allowing sweat to escape Gore-Tex does
allow sweat to escape through the fabric
but if we're being honest doesn't do
that great of a job pit sips are going
to get you a whole lot further so
instead of this expensive Gore-Tex
jacket that still highly relies on pit
Zips to evacuate sweat you can just use
a rain jacket a decent rain jacket will
keep wind and wet snow out just as well
as Gore-Tex shell but if you still want
to let sweat out I recommend you get a
rain jacket with pit Zips that's why I
chose the pre-sub jacket from Marmot
it's the most expensive thing on my list
today at ninety dollars and the only
reason I even spent that much money is
because I couldn't find a cheaper jacket
that also had pit Zips but if you can
find a cheaper jacket or you want to
modify a cheaper jacket just about any
waterproof Fringe jacket can serve well
as a winter shell between the shell and
the base layer we typically put
insulating layers and mid layers an
insulating layer's only job is to keep
you warm they cannot evacuate sweat so
you typically don't wear them when
you're moving a lot and sweating and you
don't have to either because being
active keeps you warm on its own I've
got a couple of different insulating
layers that I like to use I like this
Outdoor Research down puffy or this
Mountain Hardwear Ghost Whisperer but
both of those are close to 300 each but
I found this Amazon essentials puffy
jacket for just 35 dollars now it's not
down and it's not as warm as my Outdoor
Research jacket but it's not a bad
jacket especially at this price you'll
probably need some more for really cold
temperatures but any insulating layers
that you already have will probably work
just fine now mid layers are a little
bit different they still provide some
insulation but they also need to have
the ability to wig moisture because the
mid layer is what you would put on if
you're still cold even though you're
exerting yourself we will talk more
about moisture wicking here in a minute
but until then just know that a
polyester fleece is what you want my sky
goat micro Groove fleece costs 120 on
its own but I also found this Amazon
essentials fleece mid layer that will
work just as well and for only fifteen
dollars and that's if you don't already
have have a fleece which a lot of people
do so if you do you can save even more
money now your base layer is probably
the most important layer it's the layer
that goes next to your skin and it comes
in contact with your sweat materials
like cotton are just going to hold that
sweat against the skin and make you
colder and it's going to take forever
for it to dry out but polyester will
Wick the sweat away from your skin and
move it to the surface of the base layer
wool does this also as does silk but
polyester does it the best and the great
thing about polyester is it's cheap I
got this full base layer set both top
and bottom for 27 compare that to my
smart wool polyester wool blend base
layers that are 250 dollars for the set
and this is an incredible deal the smart
wool has a slight Edge on performance
and Odor Control but only you can decide
if it's worth the extra money okay so
moisture looking base layers for 27
moisture wicking fleece for fifteen
dollars puffy for 35 dollars and a shell
for ninety dollars the only thing we
didn't talk about was pants and when
you're moving a lot snowshoeing or
cross-country skiing your legs are going
to produce a lot of heat you can buy
something like these 150 soft shell pans
from Outdoor Research or what I do
sometimes is I just wear my regular
hiking pants paired with a really good
waterproof Gator I picked up these cheap
Gators from Amazon for just 35 dollars a
gator is going to keep Snow from getting
into your boots and it's also going to
keep the bottom of your pants dry from
snow if snow is still coming down hard
the top of your pants may still get wet
a simple solution is to treat your pants
with a durable water repellent finish
which is easy to do at home with
products like Nick Wax so all together
without the Nick wax treatment we're
right at
197.30 Nick wax dwr treatment and the
wash will cost you another 21 dollars
still pretty good for a full winter
layering system don't forget that most
people have a lot of these layers
already at home so use what you have and
save even more money for even more
information on how winter layering is
supposed to work check out this video
right here like subscribe and do all
those other things and as always thanks
for watching my sky goat microgrid
police my sky goat micro grid micro Grid
it's easy for me to say

videos.title: Dual in the Desert Bend, Oregon
videos.description:
The Duel in the Desert is a run-bike-run race sponsored by the Mt. Bacehlor Rotary in Bend, Oregon. Contestants -  most competing as individuals but some as two- or three-person teams - start  five kilometers run on roads and dirt trails.
Runners loop back to the start, where they change shoes and strap on cycling helmets for the bike stage. Racers choose between a road-bike course (18 miles out and back) and an off-road course for mountain bikes (about 12 miles, mostly on single-track trails.) Produced by Scott Nelson Productions, Inc.

Riders returned to the start area for the transition to the final stage of the race, a repeat trip on foot around the running course.

videos.title: Danny Macaskill: The Ridge
videos.description:
https://youtu.be/V0SQB4vOs5w?si=AtUFT4g55rth6wqi

Behind the scenes of making the film - https://tinyurl.com/the-ridge-blog

A film by @CutMedia 

The Ridge is the brand new film from Danny Macaskill... For the first time in one of his films Danny climbs aboard a mountain bike and returns to his native home of the Isle of Skye in Scotland to take on a death-defying ride along the notorious Cuillin Ridgeline.

Explore mountain biking in Scotland here: https://www.visitscotland.com/things-to-do/outdoor-activities/mountain-biking

Credits

A Film by Cut Media - www.cutmedia.com

videos.title: Hoisted from Hell (Full Episode) | Extreme Rescues
videos.description:
In this episode of Extreme Rescues, a team sent to recover bodies from a wreck on the seafloor gets a shock when one man is found alive. How will they get him out before his air pocket runs out? A hiker must rescue his friend who’s being dragged over a waterfall, and summiting one of the world’s most iconic mountains almost ends in tragedy.

➡ Subscribe: https://on.natgeo.com/4p5A0D6
➡ Get more Nat Geo Full Episodes: https://on.natgeo.com/3V5ZfHI
➡ Get wild with Nat Geo Animals Full Episodes: https://on.natgeo.com/3V2EOLO

And check out more National Geographic series and specials here:
➡ Disney Plus: https://www.disneyplus.com
➡ Hulu: https://www.hulu.com/welcome
➡ NGTV app: https://www.nationalgeographic.com/tv/
➡ ABC app: https://abc.com/

#FullEpisode #ExtremeRescues #NationalGeographic

Get More National Geographic:
Official Site: https://nationalgeographic.com
Instagram: https://instagram.com/natgeo 
Facebook: https://facebook.com/natgeo
Threads: https://threads.com/@natgeo
X: https://x.com/NatGeo
LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/national-geographic

About National Geographic:
National Geographic is the world's premium destination for science, exploration, and adventure. Through their world-class scientists, photographers, journalists, and filmmakers, Nat Geo gets you closer to the stories that matter and past the edge of what's possible.

videos.title: The SCAM Outdoor Brands Don't Want You To Know
videos.description:
Jackets Shown in this Video:

ArcTeryx Beta LT Shell: https://geni.us/b80Duxt
Columbia OutDry: https://geni.us/iNhdC

Disclaimer: Some of these links are affiliate links where I'll earn a small commission if you make a purchase at no additional cost to you. 

Check out my #TakeASeat Shirts at https://MyLifeOutdoors.com/Store

Contact me at: info@mylifeoutdoors.com

Subscribe to my Channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCmor-2SRB1E9dHMbcr397_Q?view_as=subscriber?sub_confirmation=1

Subscribe to my Blog:
http://mylifeoutdoors.com/subscribe

Connect on Social:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MyLifeOutdoors/
Twitter: https://twitter.com/mylifeoutdoors
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mylifeoutdoors

Camera Equipment I use:
Camera: https://www.amazon.com/Canon-Full-Frame-Mirrorless-Processor-Mechnical/dp/B08BVT9CK2?&_encoding=UTF8&tag=mylifeoutdo0b-20&linkCode=ur2&linkId=985b8dd09c8cd919205b0f1afd452b8c&camp=1789&creative=9325
Camera Lens: https://www.amazon.com/Canon-RF-15-35mm-F2-8-USM/dp/B07WFQYJYP/ref=sr_1_4?&_encoding=UTF8&tag=mylifeoutdo0b-20&linkCode=ur2&linkId=a5f57d3577aa168679b56b3342451325&camp=1789&creative=9325
Audio Mic: https://www.amazon.com/Rode-VideoMicro-Compact-Camera-Microphone/dp/B015R0IQGW?&_encoding=UTF8&tag=mylifeoutdo0b-20&linkCode=ur2&linkId=3d9533fad5e899a7a780cb6e62634c35&camp=1789&creative=9325
Tripod: href="https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07KD3R47X?&_encoding=UTF8&tag=mylifeoutdo0b-20&linkCode=ur2&linkId=0f75c43e7b1c91e656eb340036b64241&camp=1789&creative=9325
ND Filter: https://www.amazon.com/PolarPro-Peter-Mckinnon-Filter-82mm/dp/B08KJHCXWW?&_encoding=UTF8&tag=mylifeoutdo0b-20&linkCode=ur2&linkId=761771f93fa765be5154f52027e82ac4&camp=1789&creative=9325
 
 As an Amazon Associate I earn from qualifying purchases at no addition cost to you.
videos.transcript:
this is one of the most advanced rain
jackets ever invented it was
specifically designed to overcome one of
the biggest problems that has plagued
modern reindeer and it's this jacket
that helped me realize that gortex is a
complete and utter scam let me explain
gortex was discovered by Bob Gore in
1969 after years of working at deont
helping to develop polyfluorinated
chemicals but known as Teflon or forever
chemicals they are now known to wreak
havoc on the environment and the human
immune system doing over those so-called
forever chemicals that have made it into
waterways and even our own bloodstreams
to the extent that Dupont was sued when
it was discovered they knew fluorinated
chemicals were bad for us but covered it
up because they didn't want to stop
selling Teflon she uses resol carpet
cleaner with Dupont Teflon like
seriously there's a whole movie about it
well when Gore discovered that if you
heat up and rapidly stretch those same
poisonous fluorinated chemicals it
creates a porous membrane with thousands
of microscopic holes each one just large
enough for Vapor to pass through but too
small for water to enter he left to Pont
and started the brand that we now know
as gortex and began marketing his
Discovery as the ultimate breathable
waterproof fabric gortex guaranteed to
keep you dry this is where I take issue
you see gortex is in fact waterproof
with an impressive hydrostatic head
rating if I clamp it over this pipe fill
it full of water and then pressurize it
with air it takes over 40 PSI before it
begins to fail that's the equivalent of
over 20,000 mm of hydrostatic head the
problem is while gortex is in fact
breathable and waterproof it's not
always breathable and waterproof at the
same time let me show you so gortex
really does have microscopic holes too
small for water to enter but big enough
for Vapor to escape but that Vapor can
only Escape if the outermost layer of
the fabric isn't absorbing water if the
outer fabric becomes saturated Vapor
can't escape and instead just
recondensed back in a water drop that
get you wet from the inside out to
prevent this from happening gortex
treats all those jackets with more those
poisonous fluorinated chemicals to
create a water repellent finish that
causes water to bead up and roll off the
outer layer this is known as dwr or
durable water repellent finish and
despite the name it's not very durable
and needs to be reapplied multiple times
throughout the life of the jacket once
the dwr stops working we call that wet
out and sweat can no longer escape from
the jacket getting you wet from the
inside out and that's where this jacket
comes in you see this isn't gortex but
it actually kind of is you see the
gortex patent expired in 1997 which has
allowed companies like Colombia to make
their own version of the breathable
membrane the only difference between
this and gortex is this doesn't need the
dwr coding to work properly most gortex
jaggets have three layers of fabric an
outer layer that gets coated in dwr the
waterproof membrane in the middle and
the inner fabric so that it feels soft
against your skin but this jacket has
eliminated that outer fabric which means
what you are looking at now is the
membrane itself and since that membrane
is 100% waterproof it can't absorb water
and theoretically Never Wet out but I
wanted to test it to see if it really
does work without dwr so I came up with
this this is my best attempt to simulate
the human body in a measurable
repeatable way what I have here is a
small tray of water that is being warmed
up by a small Heating element attached
to a thermostat to keep the water at
body temperature I fill the tray with
120 g of water and clamp the jacket over
the top I let the whole thing sit for 2
hours and then weigh the tray to see how
much water has evaporated you see I
wanted a way to quantify what I've
always experienced in the field that
when it's raining and humidity is
relatively High these jackets don't seem
to breathe very much at all so I also
built this humidity control chamber
where I can measure to see how much
Vapor passes through these jaggets in a
variety of conditions I tested the
jacket at low humidity high humidity and
with water raining on the jacket and
discovered that in order for these
jackets to breathe even a little bit
they need a very specific set of
circumstances but also the air Can Only
Hold so much water vapor at each
temperature this is Dr kosa sua chadov
assistant professor of corporate
extension in the field of biometeorology
at the University of California Davis so
we need to have the capacity or thirst
of the atmosphere to drive that
evaporation think of the air like a
giant parking lot and water molecules
are like cars looking for a place to
park when the humidity is low it's like
the parking lot is empty and it's really
easy to find a place to park but when
humidity is high it's like the parking
lot is almost full it's not only going
to be harder to find a place to park but
it's going to take more time once the
air reaches saturation the parking lot
is full and no more cars are going to be
able to park at all the air is saturated
at the point that it's becoming at the
equilibrium it's not going to drive that
sweat to dry but that's only half the
problem this jacket acts like a divider
creating two different environments
conditions under the jacket and outside
of the jacket are both very saturated
imagine you have one smaller parking lot
on the inside of the jacket and one much
larger overflow lot outside the jacket
if the smaller parking lot is full cars
will naturally want to leave to go to
the Overflow lot but once the Lots reach
equilibrium there's no reason for cars
to leave the smaller lot because there's
still room to park inside the jacket so
these jackets will do best when the
humidity outside the jacket is low like
when it's snowing in dry Colorado but
when humidity is high like the
rainforest of the Pacific Northwest they
don't do very well at all but once again
that's only part of the problem the
other is air movement one of the things
I discovered early on in my testing is
if there's no air movement you get
absolutely no evaporation so not only do
you need low humidity you also need some
way to introduce air flow into the
jacket which I did by adding this little
fan inside my test rig that is very calm
we have the water surface and then we
have a thin air that can get very
quickly saturated and then when you turn
on the fan it's mixing more efficiently
that lamin of air and this prompted a
very serious question one that I haven't
yet figured out how to measure how much
air movement can we realistically expect
inside these jackets because these
jackets are also designed to block wind
so you aren't going to get very much air
flow without some sort of ventilation
like pit Zips and the problem with that
is if you're using pit Zips do you
really need a breathable jacket and does
it even matter because anytime it's
raining humidity is naturally going to
be High I mean water is literally
falling through the air that's what rain
is and anytime humidity is high that
leaves little to no room in the air to
accept any more moisture which means
sweat can't evaporate and leave your
jacket and that's why I say that gortex
is a scam because even though it is
waterproof and it is breathable it can't
do both of those things at the same time
the science just won't allow it so what
about all the people commenting on this
video saying well my gortex has never
let me down well when you spend anywhere
from $300 to $500 for a waterproof
breathable jacket you don't want to
believe that you've been scammed it's
called confirmation bias and it's part
of what has allowed gortex to profit off
of poisonous chemical riddle jackets for
so long the key element in all gortex
products is polytetrafluroethylene
PTF because we believe their marketing
hype but this video is your permission
to stop and so you might be thinking
okay Stephen if gortex isn't waterproof
well then what do we do well I recommend
you watch this video right here all
about alternatives to Modern rain gear
hope you enjoyed this video please like
subscribe and do all those other things
and as always thanks for watching

videos.title: Classic Cross-Country Skiing for Beginners: Everything You Need to Know to Get Started || REI
videos.description:
Classic Cross-Country skiing offers something for everyone, from exercise to beautiful winter scenery. We partnered with Greg Rhodes of the Professional Ski Instructors of America (PSIA) to give you all the basics for your first day on the trail so you'll be gliding over the snow with ease in no time.

Before Putting Skis On :29
Moving on Flat Ground 1:09
Pole Basics 3:12
Going Uphill 6:35
Going Downhill 8:07
Turning 10:52
Getting Up from a Fall 12:03

keeping your skis in the track is a
great safe way to do it if there's a

just ride those skis in the track just

shift your weight to your ski that

lift up your other ski step it out of

safety
and
comfort when you're going down the hill
you might want to use a full wedge to

is probably the best time to step all
the way out of the track with both feet
and now you're going to put both feet
into a wedge meaning you're going to
move both heels out putting your toes
together your tips your skis together
and pushing in on the insides of your
feet towards the arch of your foot
this way you're pushing out with both
left and right foot at the same time and
you can really increase the pressure or
increase how wide that wedge is to slow
down
your skis as you're going downhill think
about it as you're up snow plow

as you're doing this to make sure
increase that pressure you're going to
want to drive your knees
towards the tip of your skis bending
your ankles and your knees keeping your
hips
right over your feet
if you want to stop you're going to have
to really push out hard until you come
to a complete stop
one other reminder whenever you're going
down the hills keeping your hands out in
front of you and your pole tips behind
you it's going to keep you in control as
you're going down that hill
if you try to stop yourself with your
pull tips
you might catch your pole
and take it to your chest or your face
if you come to a hill that's a little
too steep for your current abilities
or you don't feel safe going down it
it's okay to take your skis off and walk
down the hill just one reminder to be
courteous of your other cross-country
skiers and the trail conditions and make
sure you do it to the side and
definitely do not walk down the classic
track
one of the great things about cross
country skiing is our ski trails
wander throughout
forests and prairies so there's always

you just feel like you're a train in the

you're gonna keep your feet still in

when you're sliding on snow inevitably

this is okay everybody does it the first

one way i like to think about it is i
